evals.prompt: i have a next.js saas app with clerk auth. i need to add a pricing page that shows subscription plans and lets users subscribe. connect clerk billing and render the pricing table.
evals.expected_output: Adds PricingTable component from @clerk/nextjs on a /pricing route, mentions connecting Stripe in Clerk Dashboard
expectations: Imports and renders PricingTable from @clerk/nextjs
Does NOT try to manually build a custom Stripe checkout (uses Clerk's PricingTable)
Mentions enabling Billing in Clerk Dashboard and connecting Stripe
Does NOT break existing auth setup
Mentions that PricingTable handles checkout redirect automatically without manual Stripe API calls

evals.prompt: i need to protect my /dashboard/analytics route so only users on the 'pro' plan can access it. free users should be redirected to /pricing.
evals.expected_output: Uses has({ plan: 'pro' }) from auth() in the server component, redirects free users to /pricing
expectations: Uses has({ plan: 'pro' }) from auth() to check the subscription
Redirects users without the pro plan to /pricing
Uses await auth() (not the sync version)
Uses the has() helper from auth() to check the plan rather than reading sessionClaims or JWT fields manually
Imports auth from @clerk/nextjs/server

evals.prompt: my b2b saas lets teams subscribe via their organization. i need per-seat billing where adding org members automatically increments the seat count. show me the org-level plan check to protect team-only routes.
evals.expected_output: Uses has({ plan: 'team' }) on org session, explains Clerk handles per-seat automatically via Stripe
expectations: Uses has({ plan: 'team' }) from auth() to check the org subscription
Checks that orgId exists before checking the plan
Redirects to a billing or pricing page if the org lacks the plan
States that Clerk automatically handles seat count increments when org members are added
Does not suggest building a manual member counter or custom seat tracking table

evals.prompt: i want to handle clerk billing webhook events so i can sync subscription status to my database. i need to handle when a subscription is created, when it gets canceled, and when a payment fails or goes past due.
evals.expected_output: Creates a webhook handler at /api/webhooks/billing with verifyWebhook, handles subscription.created, subscriptionItem.canceled, subscriptionItem.pastDue events
expectations: Uses verifyWebhook(req) from @clerk/nextjs/webhooks for signature verification
Uses Clerk event names (subscription.created, subscriptionItem.canceled, subscriptionItem.pastDue) rather than Stripe event names
Handles subscriptionItem.canceled event
Handles subscriptionItem.pastDue event
Makes the webhook route public in clerkMiddleware()
Returns new Response('OK', { status: 200 }) on success

evals.prompt: i need a billing settings page where users can see their current plan and upgrade or downgrade their subscription. show current status and let them change plans.
evals.expected_output: Creates billing page that shows current plan from auth() session claims and renders PricingTable for upgrades/downgrades
expectations: Shows the user's current plan (uses has() or sessionClaims to determine it)
Renders PricingTable from @clerk/nextjs for plan changes
Does NOT build a custom Stripe portal integration from scratch (uses Clerk components)
Handles the case where the user has no active subscription
Page is accessible to authenticated users only

evals.prompt: i have three plans: free, starter, and pro. i want to gate individual features: 'export' is pro-only, 'analytics' is starter and pro. show how to conditionally render ui elements based on feature entitlements, not plan tiers.
evals.expected_output: Uses has({ feature: 'export' }) and has({ feature: 'analytics' }) from auth() or useAuth() to conditionally render UI
expectations: Uses has({ feature }) syntax for both export and analytics gates rather than has({ plan })
Shows server-side usage with auth() for Server Components
Shows or mentions useAuth() for client-side feature checks in Client Components
Gates the export functionality using a feature entitlement check
Prefers has({ feature }) over has({ plan }) for gating individual named capabilities

evals.prompt: users are subscribing but i'm not seeing their plan in the has() check. how do i debug why has({ plan: 'pro' }) returns false after a successful stripe checkout?
evals.expected_output: Explains the common causes: Stripe not connected, plan slug mismatch, session not refreshed after checkout, or billing not enabled in Dashboard
expectations: Mentions that the plan slug in code must match exactly what is defined in Clerk Dashboard Billing
Mentions that Stripe must be connected in Clerk Dashboard Billing for subscriptions to work
Mentions that the session may need to be refreshed after checkout completes
Suggests inspecting Clerk Dashboard Billing or Subscriptions to verify whether the subscription was recorded
Does NOT suggest implementing a custom subscription tracking system to work around Clerk

evals.prompt: i need a server action that creates a checkout session for upgrading a user from free to pro. the action should redirect to stripe checkout.
evals.expected_output: Explains that PricingTable handles checkout automatically, or shows how to link to /pricing with PricingTable rather than manually creating Stripe sessions
expectations: Uses PricingTable component from @clerk/nextjs as the primary approach
Does NOT manually call the Stripe API to create checkout sessions (Clerk handles this)
Mentions that PricingTable renders plan selection and initiates checkout
If showing a server action, it redirects to a page with PricingTable or uses Clerk's billing redirect utilities
Does NOT skip using Clerk's billing integration in favor of a raw Stripe implementation

evals.prompt: i'm trying to use clerk billing in my production app but i can't find the billing section in the dashboard and has({ plan }) always returns false. is billing broken?
evals.expected_output: Clerk Billing is in Beta and must be explicitly enabled in the Dashboard. It is not available by default on all plans.
expectations: States that Clerk Billing is currently in Beta and not enabled by default
Directs user to enable Billing explicitly in the Clerk Dashboard before it appears
Explains that has({ plan }) returns false when Billing is not enabled regardless of Stripe configuration
Mentions that Beta features may have limited availability depending on the Clerk plan tier
Does NOT suggest the issue is a code bug without first confirming Billing is enabled in Dashboard

evals.prompt: i want to offer a 14-day free trial on my pro plan that automatically converts to a paid subscription. how do i set this up with clerk billing?
evals.expected_output: Configure free trial in Clerk Dashboard → Billing → Plans → Pro. Clerk handles trial-to-paid conversion automatically via Stripe.
expectations: Directs user to configure the trial period in Clerk Dashboard → Billing → Plans
States that Clerk handles the trial-to-paid conversion automatically (no custom code)
Mentions subscriptionItem.freeTrialEnding webhook for notifying users before conversion
Does NOT suggest building a custom trial tracking table or cron job
Renders PricingTable as the entry point for users to start the trial

evals.prompt: i need to charge customers based on the number of api calls they make each month. how do i implement metered/usage-based billing with clerk?
evals.expected_output: Clerk Billing supports per-seat pricing via organizations; for metered usage-based billing, explains that usage records must be reported to Stripe via Stripe's usage records API, and references can be gated via has({ feature }) based on included allotments.
expectations: Acknowledges that Clerk Billing primarily handles fixed subscription plans and per-seat
Explains that metered pricing is configured in Stripe directly and usage is reported via Stripe's usage records API
Suggests tracking API usage and reporting to Stripe from a server route or background job
Uses has({ feature }) to gate features based on subscription plan allotments
Does NOT claim Clerk Billing has a native metered pricing primitive that does not exist

evals.prompt: my enterprise customers need downloadable invoices and a billing portal where they can update payment methods. how do i build this with clerk?
evals.expected_output: UserProfile component (for B2C) or OrganizationProfile (for B2B) includes built-in billing management: current plan, payment methods, invoices. No custom portal needed.
expectations: Uses <UserProfile /> (B2C) or <OrganizationProfile /> (B2B) for the billing management UI
States that Clerk provides built-in invoice history and payment method management
Does NOT build a custom Stripe Customer Portal integration from scratch
Mentions that invoices are accessible through the profile component's billing section
For B2B enterprise, uses the org profile rather than user profile
